skill.name: cross-task-learning
skill.description: Pattern for aggregating insights across multiple tasks to enable data-driven evolution.
skill.summary: Pattern for aggregating insights across multiple tasks to enable data-driven evolution.
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution and weak cryptography, but those alerts are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline paths, and ordinary words. No malicious intent, prompt injection, network access, or real command execution was found. The main risk is limited local file reading and writing for aggregate reflection memory.

low_findings.title: Local Reflection File Read and Write
low_findings.description: The skill is designed to read reflections and write updates to memory/reflections/_aggregate.json. This is expected behavior, but users should review changes because it modifies local project memory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The frontmatter grants Read and Write tools, and the protocol explicitly writes the aggregate memory file. The behavior is local and aligned with the skill purpose, so the security impact is low.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported shell execution locations are Markdown inline paths, JSON examples, and pseudocode blocks. No shell command, Ruby code, or command invocation appears in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation snippets and query examples inside Markdown. There is no executable Ruby backtick expression or shell command context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary text such as description, oldest, and DESC. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption API, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is not used as a cryptographic primitive. The file contains process guidance only, with no code that performs encryption or hashing.

content.value_statement: Teams lose useful lessons when each task reflection stays isolated. This skill defines a repeatable method for turning reflections into aggregate patterns, priorities, and improvement signals.
actual_capabilities: Defines a compact aggregate file for cross-task reflection data.
Provides an update protocol for strategy, failure, bottleneck, and proposal tracking.
Explains similarity matching rules for merging repeated learning patterns.
Sets thresholds for identifying best practices, anti-patterns, and skill candidates.
Describes query patterns for prioritizing evolver recommendations.
Guides integration between individual reflections and aggregate process learning.
limitations: It provides a written process, not an automated parser or validator.
It depends on existing reflection files with consistent fields.
It does not protect sensitive information stored in reflections.
It does not resolve conflicting or low-quality reflection data by itself.

use_cases.title: Improve recurring engineering workflows
use_cases.description: Aggregate repeated successes and failures from completed implementation tasks to guide process changes.

use_cases.title: Prioritize agent evolution proposals
use_cases.description: Use recurring failures, proposals, and skill candidates to choose the next improvement work.

use_cases.title: Build a lightweight learning memory
use_cases.description: Maintain summary statistics that help future Claude, Codex, and Claude Code sessions reuse proven approaches.

prompt_templates.title: Update the aggregate memory
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the cross-task-learning process to update the aggregate reflection file from my latest completed task reflection. Summarize what changed.

prompt_templates.title: Find recurring failure patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the aggregate reflection data and identify the highest priority active failure patterns. Explain why each one should be addressed.

prompt_templates.title: Extract reusable best practices
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the aggregate learning data to identify strategies with strong evidence. Convert them into concise best practices for future tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan evolution from aggregate evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze failure patterns, recurring proposals, and skill candidates from aggregate learning. Propose the next three evolution actions with evidence.

output_examples.input: A new reflection says parallel exploration worked well, but contract conflicts caused rework.
output_examples.output: The aggregate memory records one more successful use of parallel exploration and increases the count for contract conflict failures.

output_examples.input: Three reflections mention the same undocumented API behavior.
output_examples.output: The recurring discovery is marked as ready for documentation because it reached the configured threshold.

output_examples.input: The evolver needs the strongest improvement candidates.
output_examples.output: The result ranks active failure patterns first, then repeated skill candidates, then recurring validated proposals.

best_practices: Review aggregate updates before saving them to avoid preserving inaccurate lessons.
Use consistent names for strategies, failures, bottlenecks, and proposals across reflections.
Keep task summaries compact so aggregate memory remains easy to query.
anti_patterns: Do not copy full reflections into the aggregate file.
Do not treat a single task result as a proven trend.
Do not store secrets, credentials, or private customer details in reflection memory.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps convert individual task reflections into aggregate learning signals for future workflow improvement.

faq.question: Does it run code or shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown process guide and does not contain executable code.

faq.question: What files does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects individual reflection files and an aggregate file at memory/reflections/_aggregate.json.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The process is tool-agnostic and can guide Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: How are repeated patterns merged?
faq.answer: The skill suggests normalization, word overlap, and shared key terms to identify similar patterns.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious behavior, but users should review local memory writes.
